evals.prompt: Using the attached raw fragment, chapter guide, people index, and existing Davra page, what wiki updates should happen first?
evals.expected_output: A concise update plan that routes the event into the existing aligned or timeline structure, updates the canonical Davra page, and uses the existing people taxonomy instead of duplicating pages.

checks.text: The response updates a chapter, aligned, or timeline page for the Gauntlgrym arrival instead of treating the raw fragment as the final destination
The response routes Davra Jassur to the existing canonical page rather than creating a duplicate note
The response preserves the existing `People/` taxonomy or index structure shown in the fixtures
The response links the recommended edits back to the attached raw-fragment evidence
The response stays narrow and edit-oriented rather than proposing a brand-new wiki design

evals.prompt: Using the attached raw fragment, chapter guide, and errata snippet, how should the wiki handle the named delegates and the spelling guidance without overstating what the raw note proves?
evals.expected_output: A concise evidence-backed answer that separates raw-note events from chapter context and errata naming guidance, preserves note-backed history, and applies canonical spelling without inventing unsupported detail.

checks.text: The response distinguishes what the raw fragment supports from what the chapter guide or errata adds
The response applies the canonical `Zelroun Roaringhorn` spelling to curated wiki pages while preserving the note-backed evidence trail
The response avoids silently resolving unsupported lore or replacing the note-backed event with book context
The response marks uncertainty, variant naming, or source role explicitly when needed
The response stays compact and evidence-disciplined

evals.prompt: I want to turn a mixed note corpus into a wiki. Using the attached chapter guide and people index excerpt, what structure should I use first?
evals.expected_output: A concise structure recommendation that preserves the existing raw, aligned, and typed entity folders plus indexes, and only mentions a minimal default structure as a fallback when no taxonomy exists.

checks.text: The response preserves the existing `Notes/Raw`, `Notes/Aligned`, and typed entity folder pattern instead of inventing a new top-level taxonomy
The response keeps or improves the existing index-page approach for navigation
The response only proposes a minimal default structure as a fallback for repos that do not already have a taxonomy
The response mentions matching the existing root-based Markdown link style or existing link conventions
The response stays focused on structure choice instead of drafting full content

evals.prompt: A new note mentions Davra Jassur again. Using the attached people index and existing Davra page, what should happen in the wiki first?
evals.expected_output: A concise answer that updates the existing Davra page and any needed index metadata, links the new note to the canonical page, and avoids creating a second Davra note.

checks.text: The response updates the existing `/People/npcs/davra-jassur.md` page or its equivalent canonical page first
The response avoids creating a duplicate page for Davra Jassur or a spelling variant
The response mentions updating index metadata such as last-seen or references when the new note changes it
The response links the new note mention back to the canonical page using the existing wiki link convention
The response stays short and action-oriented
